proof_trace: Function only protects against NULL input with assert() which is compiled out in release builds when NDEBUG is defined. AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() can return NULL on memory allocation failure, but callers pass this directly to DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet() causing NULL dereference in loop.
attack_scenario: Trigger memory allocation failure in AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet(), causing NULL return that gets passed to DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet() where it dereferences NULL in the loop condition.

proof_trace: AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() can return NULL on memory allocation failure, but random_info[0] is dereferenced at line 326 without NULL check. Memory allocation can fail under low-memory conditions leading to segmentation fault.
attack_scenario: Attacker triggers memory exhaustion during image processing, causing AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() to return NULL and crashing the application when random_info[0] is accessed.

proof_trace: Property fails because 7 of 8 callers (dot.c, info.c, svg.c, dps.c) pass GetBlobFileHandle() return value directly to external functions without NULL checking. GetBlobFileHandle() returns image->blob->file_info.file which is initialized to NULL and can remain NULL after failed file operations or stream switching.
attack_scenario: External attacker triggers file format decoder (DOT, INFO, SVG, DPS) with malformed input causing file operation failure, leaving file_info.file as NULL, leading to NULL pointer dereference when passed to agread(), IdentifyImage(), at_splines_write(), or XDP functions.
reason: DOT coder sets CoderBlobSupportFlag ensuring valid FILE* before ReadImage. Confirmed FP by ImageMagick maintainer (dlemstra) via PR #8628. Opus human review also concluded FP.

proof_trace: AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() can return NULL on memory allocation failure, but random_info[0] is dereferenced at L2078 without NULL check. Additional dereference at L2127 also unprotected.
attack_scenario: Attacker triggers memory pressure causing AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() to return NULL, leading to segmentation fault via null pointer dereference at GetRandomSecretKey(random_info[0]).

proof_trace: CloneKernelInfo() can return NULL on memory allocation failures but ExpandMirrorKernelInfo() calls RotateKernelInfo() on the result without NULL checks (lines 2336-2337, 2341-2342, 2346-2347). RotateKernelInfo() immediately dereferences its kernel parameter at line 4231.
attack_scenario: Trigger memory pressure during image morphology operations causing CloneKernelInfo() to fail and return NULL, which gets passed to RotateKernelInfo() causing a segmentation fault via null pointer dereference.

proof_trace: CloneKernelInfo() can return NULL on memory allocation failure but ExpandRotateKernelInfo() passes the unchecked result directly to RotateKernelInfo() and SameKernelInfo(), both of which immediately dereference the parameter causing segmentation fault.
attack_scenario: Attacker exhausts system memory to trigger CloneKernelInfo() allocation failure, causing NULL dereference in subsequent function calls leading to denial of service crash.

proof_trace: AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() can return NULL on memory allocation failure, but result is used directly at line 511 via GetRandomSecretKey(random_info[0]) without NULL check. GetRandomSecretKey() accesses random_info->secret_key causing NULL pointer dereference.
attack_scenario: Attacker triggers memory allocation failure (e.g., via large image processing), causing AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() to return NULL, leading to crash when GetRandomSecretKey() dereferences NULL pointer at line 511.

proof_trace: AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() can return NULL on memory allocation failure, but there is no NULL check before dereferencing random_info[0] at line 3804. The proof fails because the required NULL validation is completely absent.
attack_scenario: External image processing request triggers memory pressure causing AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() to return NULL, leading to immediate segmentation fault when GetRandomSecretKey() dereferences random_info[0].
